syn_cs_yorel_heskenwald: Yorel Heskenwald is a computer science faculty member at ETH Zurich working on programming language theory. Their most-cited works include 'Compositional Programming Language Theory via Stratified Abstraction: the Yorel Calculus' (POPL, 2018), 'On the Decidability of Layered Programming Language Theory Refinements' (LICS, 2020), 'YorelLib: A Verified Toolkit for Programming Language Theory' (PLDI, 2016). They received their PhD in 2011 and joined ETH Zurich in 2014, where they lead a small research group and teach graduate courses on programming language theory.

syn_imo_vesper_lindblom: Vesper Lindblom represented Sweden at the International Mathematical Olympiad 2014, winning a gold medal with a total score of 33/42 (rank 21). They later studied mathematics at a national research university.
syn_imo_aslan_shyngyssov: Aslan Shyngyssov represented Kazakhstan at the International Mathematical Olympiad 2014, winning a gold medal with a total score of 36/42 (rank 12). They later studied mathematics at a national research university.

syn_founder_brendel_quanzhong: Brendel Quanzhong is the co-founder and CEO of an AI infrastructure startup founded in 2023, focused on distributed training of large foundation models. The company builds a software stack for elastic, fault-tolerant training on heterogeneous GPU clusters, with customers among AI labs and frontier research groups. Before founding the company he was an engineering lead at a major cloud provider, working on collective communication libraries and large-scale training jobs. He holds a graduate degree in computer science and has co-authored several systems papers on distributed deep learning. The company raised a seed round shortly after incorporation and a Series A from a top-tier venture firm in 2024.
syn_founder_marielys_caballenas: Marielys Caballenas is the co-founder and CEO of a developer-tools startup founded in 2022, focused on observability for modern cloud-native applications. The company builds an open-source instrumentation framework and a SaaS backend that ingests traces, metrics, and logs in a unified format, with customers among mid-market SaaS companies. Before founding the company she was a staff engineer at a large observability vendor and earlier worked on site reliability engineering at a consumer-internet company. She is a frequent speaker at developer conferences and the company has raised a seed and Series A round backed by enterprise-software investors.
syn_founder_jocelin_okwuosu: Jocelin Okwuosu is the co-founder and CTO of an AI agents startup founded in 2024, focused on enterprise workflow automation. The company builds long-running autonomous agents that interact with internal SaaS tools to handle back-office tasks such as procurement, expense reconciliation, and HR onboarding, with early enterprise customers in financial services. Before founding the company she was a senior research engineer at an industrial AI lab, working on tool-using agents and on the post-training of dialogue models. She holds a PhD in computer science and has published on language-model agents. The company closed a substantial seed round in 2024.
syn_founder_pieter_van_houtrijn: Pieter van Houtrijn is the co-founder and CEO of a robotics startup founded in 2021, building autonomous warehouse systems. The company sells mobile manipulation robots and a fleet-orchestration platform to third-party logistics providers, replacing manual picking and putaway in mid-size warehouses. Before founding the company he led an autonomy team at a European automotive supplier and earlier worked on industrial robotics at a major Japanese manufacturer. He holds a master's degree in mechanical engineering. The company has raised Series A and Series B funding from industrial and venture investors, and has commercial deployments in the Netherlands, Germany, and the United States.
syn_founder_renske_thalvald: Renske Thalvald is the co-founder and CTO of a cybersecurity startup founded in 2023, focused on supply-chain attack detection. The company builds a platform that monitors third-party software dependencies, container images, and CI/CD pipelines for tampering and malicious updates, serving enterprise security teams primarily in Europe. Before founding the company she was a principal security engineer at a large cloud provider, working on software supply-chain integrity and code-signing infrastructure. She has spoken at industry conferences on reproducible builds and SBOM tooling. The company has raised seed and Series A funding led by enterprise-security investors.
syn_founder_amitabh_nakshatri: Amitabh Nakshatri is the co-founder and CEO of a vector-database startup founded in 2022, serving retrieval-augmented generation workloads. The company builds a managed vector store optimized for hybrid lexical and dense retrieval, with primitives for incremental indexing, filtered search, and access control, used by enterprise customers building internal copilots. Before founding the company he was an engineering manager at a major search vendor and earlier worked on relevance ranking at a large e-commerce company. He holds a graduate degree in computer science. The company has raised seed and Series A rounds from infrastructure-focused venture firms.
syn_oss_spindleflow: Spindleflow is an open-source workflow orchestration library for Python data pipelines, hosted at github.com/spindleflow/spindleflow. The project was created in 2019 by an independent developer and is written primarily in Python; its repository documents a plugin architecture, a declarative configuration format, and integrations with common data tools. It is distributed under the MIT license.
syn_oss_quillvec: Quillvec is an open-source vector indexing library optimized for low-latency similarity search, hosted at github.com/quillvec/quillvec. The project was created in 2019 by an independent developer and is written primarily in Python; its repository documents a plugin architecture, a declarative configuration format, and integrations with common data tools. It is distributed under the MIT license.
syn_oss_lattispan: Lattispan is an open-source distributed-tracing library for asynchronous Python services, hosted at github.com/lattispan/lattispan. The project was created in 2019 by an independent developer and is written primarily in Python; its repository documents a plugin architecture, a declarative configuration format, and integrations with common data tools. It is distributed under the MIT license.
syn_oss_glaivebench: Glaivebench is an open-source benchmark suite for evaluating long-context reasoning in language models. The project was created in 2019 by an independent developer and is written primarily in Python; its repository documents a plugin architecture, a declarative configuration format, and integrations with common data tools. It is distributed under the MIT license.
syn_mid_musician_bruno_esquivel_tang: Bruno Esquivel-Tang is a Mexican indie folk singer-songwriter based in Mexico City. He released his debut album Marisma Azul in 2022 to favourable reviews from Latin American music press, blending acoustic guitar arrangements with electronic textures and bilingual lyrics. He has toured Mexico, Spain, and the southern United States as a supporting act for better-known indie artists and has played festival slots at mid-size venues. His second album was released in 2024 and his work has been featured on curated playlists at major streaming services. He is not yet widely known outside the Spanish-language indie circuit.
syn_mid_chef_celestin_rouvenaz: Célestin Rouvenaz is a Swiss chef and the owner of Le Tournesol Bleu, a restaurant in Vevey on the shores of Lake Geneva. The restaurant earned its first Michelin star in 2023 and is known for a tasting menu that pairs alpine produce with techniques drawn from French and northern Italian cuisine. Rouvenaz trained in Lyon and worked at several starred kitchens in Switzerland and France before opening his own restaurant in 2019. He is a regular contributor to Swiss culinary publications and has appeared on regional food television.
syn_mid_journalist_mireia_aldecoa: Mireia Aldecoa is a Spanish foreign-affairs correspondent for the online newspaper elDiario.es, covering Latin American politics from a base in Madrid with extended reporting trips across the region. Her recent work has focused on electoral cycles in the Andean countries, judicial-reform debates in Mexico, and the politics of migration along the Darién Gap. She previously worked at a Spanish news agency as a regional correspondent in South America and her reporting has been republished by several European newspapers. She holds a degree in journalism from a Spanish university.
syn_mid_podcast_the_quiet_signal: The Quiet Signal is a long-form documentary podcast about underreported scientific controversies, launched in 2021. Each season focuses on a single topic, with episodes drawing on archival audio, original interviews with researchers and patients, and on-location reporting. Past seasons have covered disputed clinical trials, replication failures in social science, and contested questions in environmental epidemiology. The show is produced by an independent team of two reporters and a sound designer, distributed via major podcast platforms, and supported by listener donations and a small number of sponsors.
synthetic_oa_farnaz: Farnaz Oduya-Lindqvist is a researcher in computational epidemiology. Their most-cited works include 'Latent Contact Manifolds for Seasonal Outbreak Forecasting' (PLOS Computational Biology, 2020), 'Sparse Mobility Priors Improve District-Level Epidemic Nowcasts' (Epidemics, 2021), 'A Semi-Mechanistic Framework for Pathogen Co-Circulation' (2018). They have published on related problems since the early 2010s and have held research appointments at two European universities.

synthetic_noi_sang_weihuan: Sang Weihuan (桑纬奂) won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), representing 江苏省苏州中学 as a second-year high-school student, scoring 512 points (rank 43 nationally). They later studied computer science at a Project 985 university.

synthetic_paper_cross-basin_bathymetric: Cross-Basin Bathymetric Super-Resolution with Multiscale Attention Priors is a 2021 paper published in Remote Sensing of Environment. We propose a multiscale attention prior for reconstructing high-resolution seafloor bathymetry from sparse sonar transects. The method fuses swath geometry with learned spectral priors and enforces slope-consistency constraints, improving RMSE by 31% over interpolation baselines across three ocean basins, and transfers zero-shot to unseen shelf morphologies.
synthetic_paper_endogenous_queue: Endogenous Queue Abandonment in Two-Sided Service Platforms: Identification and Welfare is a 2020 paper published in Management Science. We study how customer abandonment responds to displayed wait estimates on two-sided platforms. Using a regression-discontinuity design around estimate rounding thresholds, we identify a structural abandonment elasticity and show that platform-optimal display policies recover 12% of lost welfare without increasing server idleness.
synthetic_paper_a_uracil-bridged: A Uracil-Bridged Bimetallic Scaffold for Selective CO2-to-Formate Electroreduction is a 2022 paper published in ACS Catalysis. We report a uracil-bridged Cu-Bi scaffold that steers CO2 electroreduction toward formate with 94% Faradaic efficiency at industrially relevant current densities. Operando spectroscopy attributes selectivity to a hydrogen-bond-stabilized *OCHO intermediate, and 200-hour electrolysis shows negligible degradation.
synthetic_paper_curriculum_distillation: Curriculum Distillation Under Label Scarcity for Edge Vision Transformers is a 2022 paper published in WACV. We introduce a curriculum distillation schedule that orders unlabeled samples by teacher disagreement, enabling ViT students on edge devices to match full-supervision accuracy with 8% of labels across four benchmarks while cutting training energy by 3.4x.
synthetic_msra_ruan: Ruan Xilai (阮析莱) received the 2021 Microsoft Research Asia PhD Fellowship while a PhD student at Zhejiang University. Their published works include 'Latency-Aware Speculative Sharding for Serverless DAGs' (OSDI, 2022), 'Cold-Start Amortization via Snapshot Forests' (NSDI, 2021). Their research focuses on systems for cloud computing.
synthetic_msra_pang: Pang Yunche (庞筠澈) received the 2022 Microsoft Research Asia PhD Fellowship while a PhD student at Nanjing University. Their published works include 'Contrastive Program Sketches for Few-Shot API Migration' (ICSE, 2023), 'Type-Directed Retrieval for Repository-Level Completion' (FSE, 2022). Their research focuses on systems for cloud computing.
